competences: Analyze written documents and media sources
contenus: Statistical overview of poverty, homelessness, and abortion in the U.S.
Analysis of news articles, magazines, and internet reports
Debate on the pros and cons of abortion and the death penalty
chapters.summary: Students explore major social challenges in the U.S., examining data, media representations, and engaging in structured debates on abortion and capital punishment.

competences: Evaluate arguments for and against the death penalty
contenus: Comparative analysis of death penalty laws in different countries
Human rights implications of capital punishment
Student debate: Adoption of the death penalty vs. life imprisonment
chapters.summary: Through comparative study and debate, students critically assess the death penalty's ethical, legal, and human rights dimensions.

competences: Propose realistic solutions to social problems
Analyze urban vs rural living conditions
contenus: Survey design and data collection on homelessness in an urban area
Presentation of findings and proposed interventions
Discussion on advantages and disadvantages of city life
chapters.summary: Students gather first‑hand data on Haitian homelessness, analyze urban challenges, and develop actionable solutions to improve community well‑being.

competences: Identify and use adjectives in correct positions
contenus: Formation of the possessive case (’s, s’) in various contexts
Exercises on comparative and superlative forms
chapters.summary: Students strengthen foundational grammar skills, enabling clearer expression of descriptions, ownership, and comparisons in English.

competences: Construct relative clauses to add detail
contenus: Usage of auxiliary verbs (do/does, have/has, will, can, etc.)
Practice forming question tags in dialogue
Building relative clauses to modify nouns
chapters.summary: This unit deepens students’ grammatical precision, supporting more complex sentence structures and nuanced communication.

competences: Discuss freedom of choice in different societies
contenus: Cultural contexts: Haiti, U.S., and other selected countries
Historical evolution of abortion laws and public opinion
Student presentations on cultural influences and personal reflections
chapters.summary: Students investigate how cultural, religious, and legal factors shape attitudes toward abortion, fostering critical thinking and respectful dialogue.
